summary: Relocates the check:docs gate script to its canonical .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh, widens artifactSchema to track the installed-artifact contract (canonical file locations, not just project.json), settles the deferred PR #17 ledger entries, and adds the MATERIA.md § Design tool section (the design-tool capability seam, default none).

changes.summary: The binding check:docs gate script (.materia/scripts/check-docs.sh) must be present; a repo predating it fails the gate.
changes.manualMigration: Copy the gate script from the installed plugin scaffold (scaffold/.materia/scripts/check-docs.sh) to .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh, and remove any stale scripts/check-docs.mjs (the artifact the .sh replaced) if present. Then update any consumer references that still name the old checker: the MATERIA.md § Gate row, package scripts (package.json), CI workflows, settings allowlists, and present-state docs (the dated run folders under docs/specs/ and docs/bugs/_reports/ are frozen and exempt). Or run /materia:migrate --apply, which installs it via the install-check-docs migration and surfaces those stale references (referenceFollowUps) for its skill to sweep before re-running the check:docs gate.
changes.notes: Historical drift, recorded now at release time: the check:docs gate script originated before PR #10 as scripts/check-docs.mjs, which the portable POSIX-sh check-docs.sh later replaced; a pre-#10 dogfood repo has neither at the canonical location and fails the binding gate. Classified required (operator decision): the fix is mechanical and one-command. This entry's schema-3 window (1,3] reaches BOTH untracked-legacy and schema-2 installs, so a missing gate yields action-needed uniformly regardless of tracking vintage; the adopted-drift filter spares any repo that already carries the script (check-docs-sh-present ok). Renamed from PR #17's suggested id 0.2.0-check-docs-sh: it lives in 0.3.0.json (0.2.0.json, a shipped file, is never retro-edited) so the window reaches untracked repos too.

changes.summary: The check:docs gate script's canonical location moved from scripts/check-docs.sh to .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh.
changes.manualMigration: Move scripts/check-docs.sh to .materia/scripts/check-docs.sh by hand (git mv preserves history and any local edits), then stamp artifact schema 3 in .materia/project.json. Then update any consumer references that still name the old path: the MATERIA.md § Gate row, package scripts (package.json), CI workflows, settings allowlists, and present-state docs (the dated run folders under docs/specs/ and docs/bugs/_reports/ are frozen and exempt). Or run /materia:migrate --apply, which relocates via the install-check-docs migration (rename in place, then stamp) and surfaces those stale references (referenceFollowUps) for its skill to sweep before re-running the check:docs gate. If the script is absent at BOTH locations, this item does not apply — 0.3.0-check-docs-sh-gate covers installing it (same migration).
changes.notes: The old root scripts/check-docs.sh location keeps working: skills no longer hardcode the gate path — they resolve check:docs from the installed repo's own MATERIA.md § Gate row, so a mixed-vintage install whose § Gate still names the root path runs fine. Relocation is recommended (tidiness + the canonical-layout contract), not required. Shares the install-check-docs migration with 0.3.0-check-docs-sh-gate (buildPlan dedups it). Consumer-staleness lesson (gymii, the first real migration): relocating the artifact cleanly still left the repo's OWN consumers of the old path stale — a broken check:docs gate behind a healthy doctor, because doctor certifies the artifact, not its references. migrate now runs a deterministic reference scan and reports the stale consumers (referenceFollowUps); its skill sweeps the auto-fixable ones (never the frozen dated run folders) and re-runs the gate.

changes.summary: MATERIA.md § Forge gains a merge-strategy knob (the PR-merge method the pipelines use).
changes.detectionNotes: The knob is optional prose inside MATERIA.md § Forge; its absence is indistinguishable from a repo that deliberately omits it, and no default is wrong — so no doctor check can honestly flag it as drift.
changes.manualMigration: Add the merge-strategy knob to MATERIA.md § Forge by hand, matching the current scaffold's § Forge subsection.

changes.summary: The bundled scaffold queue READMEs (proposed-specs / bug-reports) gained clarifying prose.
changes.detectionNotes: A prose refinement inside materialized queue README docs, which the user repo freely edits; there is no canonical byte-string to diff against, so no doctor check can detect it without false positives.

changes.summary: MATERIA.md gains a § Design tool section — the capability seam (author/read/export/tokens/reference) for external design tools the pipeline authors on over MCP; defaults to none.
changes.detectionNotes: A new named prose section in MATERIA.md, which the user repo freely edits; skills that consume the section treat a missing section as none, so absence is behaviorally identical to the shipped default and cannot honestly be flagged as drift.
changes.manualMigration: Add the § Design tool section to MATERIA.md by hand, matching the current scaffold's section (heading, slots, capability meanings, degradation rules); set Tool to none unless the repo actually connects a design tool over MCP.
changes.notes: Nothing consumes the section at this plugin version — it is the seam the later design-stage releases gate on (the consume-missing-as-none rule is a prospective contract on those consumers, not a description of current behavior). Adding it ahead of need is deliberate: later releases edit a known home instead of inventing one.

changes.summary: The scaffold review-angles README gained a short cross-reference to the reviewer-calibration guidance the ship-spec review machinery provides centrally (severity rubric, structured finding shape, verify-before-reporting).
changes.detectionNotes: A single cross-reference line in a materialized doc the user repo may edit; its absence is not mechanically distinguishable from an intentional local edit, so no doctor check can flag it honestly.
